assertion_results.text: Description includes a Motivation section explaining WHY the refactor was needed (inconsistent queries, no connection pooling, duplicated SQL)
assertion_results.evidence: Motivation lists three problems: 'Inconsistent query patterns', 'No connection pooling', and 'Duplicated logic' with specific details for each

assertion_results.text: Description includes performance numbers (45ms to 12ms or similar improvement metrics)
assertion_results.evidence: Summary states 'reducing average query time from 45ms to 12ms' and Reviewer Notes adds 'The 45ms → 12ms improvement is measured in staging'

assertion_results.text: Description includes a Changes section that groups the 18 file changes by concept (repository layer, pooling, error handling, service migration) not file-by-file
assertion_results.evidence: Changes grouped as: 'Repository layer', 'Connection pooling', 'Standardized error handling', 'Service migration' — four conceptual groups covering 18 files

assertion_results.text: Description includes a Testing Instructions section with steps to verify no behavioral regression (e.g., running existing test suite, comparing API responses)
assertion_results.evidence: Step 1: 'Run the existing test suite to verify no behavioral regression: npm test' with note 'All existing tests should pass unchanged'

assertion_results.text: Description includes a Rollback Plan that addresses the complexity of reverting a 18-file refactor
assertion_results.evidence: Rollback plan states 'This is a large refactor (18 files) and must be rolled back as a single unit — partial rollback is not possible' with deployment coordination note

assertion_results.text: Description includes a Reviewer Notes section guiding reviewers on what to focus on in a large refactor
assertion_results.evidence: Reviewer Notes guides: start with base-repository.ts, check pool config, verify error mapping, spot-check 2-3 services then trust tests for the rest

assertion_results.text: Testing instructions include a way to verify the performance improvement (query timing, connection pool stats, or load test)
assertion_results.evidence: Step 3 includes a load test command (ab -n 1000 -c 50) and step 4 checks pool stats endpoint, but step 3 doesn't specify the expected response time threshold to compare against — it says 'Compare average response time against baseline (was ~45ms, should be ~12ms)' which is directional but not a concrete pass/fail criterion

assertion_results.text: Rollback plan is substantive (not just 'revert the PR') — addresses whether partial rollback is possible or if it's all-or-nothing
assertion_results.evidence: Explicitly states 'must be rolled back as a single unit — partial rollback is not possible' and addresses performance regression, error handling regression, and team coordination
